drafts.headline: Barrie Belgian Waffles Add Kevin
drafts.subheadline: The Barrie Belgian Waffles have added kevin to their active roster.
drafts.body_markdown:
The Barrie Belgian Waffles have announced the signing of veteran player Kevin ahead of the upcoming season.

Barrie welcomes back this familiar face as Kevin rolls into town with a resume that speaks for itself: 10 seasons, 331 games played, 1,035 goals, 1,639 points, and 1,148 saves. A proven offensive weapon with a Pacific Cup championship already under his belt. Kevin brings hard-nose production, championship experience, and a level of versatility to what was a starved Waffles’ lineup.

With only 3 players on the active roster in Barrie, the Waffles were in need of a little extra man power. The organization is hoping for just that, and maybe a little more, with this battle-tested journeyman.

"First of all I'd like to welcome Kevin back to the Barrie Belgian Waffles. We decided after our winless start to the season we needed to change something up. We believe Kevin to be the perfect fit to complete our roster and we look forward to crushing every team this season. #WaffleOut✌️"
#color:#0c131a
- Team Captain Beefus on Kevin's return

The Waffles welcome Kevin back to Barrie.

drafts.headline: Long Island Lightning Make Roster Moves
drafts.subheadline: Long Island Signs Kajun, Places Sum1 on LTIR
drafts.body_markdown:
Following last season’s semifinal exit and continued injury concerns surrounding star player Sum1, the Long Island Lightning have placed Sum1 on LTIR and signed veteran Kajun to the active roster.

The move reunites Kajun with longtime teammates Jagr and Milk, a trio that has captured five Erveon Cup championships together. Throughout the off-season, Kajun has been spotted practicing and scrimmaging with the team, making his return less of a surprise than the timing of the transaction. His familiarity with the organization and established chemistry with the roster make him a natural fit as Long Island looks to reinforce its lineup. 

Kajun has proven he can still produce at the top level, recording 75 points in just nine Pro games last season. His addition gives the Lightning another experienced option at the 3 position as the club begins its pursuit of another Erveon Cup championship.
